verdict_reason: Measurement infrastructure works (ECE=0.0996 passes <0.15 gate; calibration diagnostics + A/B + block-bootstrap persisted cleanly; backtest improved AUC 0.930 vs 0.901 and Brier 0.200 vs 0.207); BUT two concerns: (1) test-set ensemble PR-AUC dropped 17pct vs baseline (0.1601 vs 0.193, >15pct discard threshold) after strict-vintage retraining -- the first update_job-only run matched baseline exactly (0.193), so this is retraining-variance on fresh data, not an A1 bug, but still trips the gate; (2) block-bootstrap CIs on backfilled history collapse to the 0.01 floor on 128 of 134 labeled rows, so the new CI method cannot demonstrate empirical coverage without further work.
notes: Block-bootstrap CI method works end-to-end but produces degenerate (floor) intervals on most backfilled historical predictions because the per-replicate meta-isotonic often maps a stable test-ensemble value to a single calibrated value (width~0). Non-trivial widths appear only in 6 of 134 labeled rows. For those rows, outcome coverage is 0/6 on 0/1 outcome (expected since CI is on probability, not outcome) - but width does expand meaningfully around COVID (CI=[0.62, 0.99] at 2020-03). Ensemble calibrator winner is 'sigmoid' (beta and isotonic tied on most models; ensemble prefers sigmoid). A1 is measurement-only so default calibrator stays isotonic per builder; consider promoting sigmoid at ensemble level in follow-up. ALFRED mean-abs-gap 5.54pct (vs baseline 4.3pct) is within acceptable range given fresh vintage data. Monitor's calibration_quality check correctly SKIPs due to insufficient labeled rolling window (expected in stable period). Key follow-up: improve block-bootstrap CI to produce meaningful intervals during stable expansion periods (currently collapses to 0.01 floor).
